evals.prompt: hey, I need BDD tests for my Button component. it has variants (primary, secondary, danger), can be disabled, and shows a loading spinner when isLoading is true. the component is at src/components/Button/Button.tsx
evals.expected_output: A .feature file with scenarios covering each variant, disabled state, and loading state, plus a .steps.tsx file implementing those scenarios with RTL. Tests should pass.
assertions: A file src/components/Button/Button.feature is created
A file src/components/Button/Button.steps.tsx is created
The .feature file contains at least 3 distinct Scenario blocks
The .feature file uses parameterized steps with quoted strings for variants (e.g. "primary", "secondary")
The .feature file does NOT contain any CSS selectors, data-testid attributes, or class names
The .steps.tsx file imports from @testing-library/react and @amiceli/vitest-cucumber
The .steps.tsx file uses loadFeature with the correct path to the .feature file
The .steps.tsx file uses describeFeature, not describe or it
The .steps.tsx file uses AfterEachScenario with cleanup(), not beforeEach
All parameterized step callbacks use _ctx as first argument
Step matchers use {string} or {int} templates, not regex
npm test passes for the Button component

evals.prompt: I already have BDD tests for my Modal component but I need to add scenarios for: 1) closing the modal when pressing Escape, 2) trapping focus inside the modal. The existing files are at src/components/Modal/Modal.feature and src/components/Modal/Modal.steps.tsx
evals.expected_output: The existing .feature and .steps.tsx files are updated with new scenarios appended, without overwriting existing scenarios. Tests pass.
assertions: The skill reads the existing Modal.feature before modifying it
The skill reads the existing Modal.steps.tsx before modifying it
Previously existing scenarios in Modal.feature are preserved unchanged
Two new Scenario blocks are added to Modal.feature (Escape key and focus trap)
Corresponding step definitions are added to Modal.steps.tsx
The new steps follow the same code style as existing steps
npm test passes for the Modal component

evals.prompt: create bdd tests for the SearchInput component at src/components/SearchInput/SearchInput.tsx — it renders an input, calls onSearch when you type and press Enter, and shows a clear button when there's text. I think we already have some shared steps for input interactions
evals.expected_output: A .feature and .steps.tsx that reuse shared steps where applicable instead of duplicating them. The skill should check sharedSteps.ts and existing feature files first.
assertions: The skill reads src/test/sharedSteps.ts before writing step definitions
The skill checks existing .feature files for reusable step patterns
The .feature file is created at src/components/SearchInput/SearchInput.feature
The .steps.tsx file imports and uses shared steps where matching patterns exist
The .feature file contains scenarios for: rendering, typing + Enter, and clear button
No step definition duplicates functionality already available in sharedSteps.ts
The scenario-to-requirement mapping is presented to the user before writing files
